summary: Enumeration-driven server-migration bug hunt: per-intent contract trace + orthogonal lenses + adversarial verify + loop-until-dry
logs: Detection: 30 per-intent contract traces + 7 orthogonal lenses
Dredge round 2: 6 fresh raised, 6 confirmed (total confirmed 33)
Dredge round 3: 4 fresh raised, 4 confirmed (total confirmed 37)
Dredge round 4: 5 fresh raised, 5 confirmed (total confirmed 42)
Dredge round 5: 5 fresh raised, 5 confirmed (total confirmed 47)

confirmed.title: create-route REMOTE rejects the default "any cargo" route: server validation treats explicit null filterResource as invalid
confirmed.description: The client gateway REMOTE path sends create-route with filterResource set to an explicit null whenever the player picks the default "any (priority)" cargo option (the FIRST and default dropdown entry). The server's create-route handler validates filterResource with `if (filterResource !== undefined && typeof filterResource !== 'string') return error`. For a JSON-transmitted null, `null !== undefined` is true and `typeof null !== 'string'` is true, so the handler returns error 'filterResource must be a string when present' and never creates the route. In LOCAL mode the identical request succeeds (the pure fn createRouteFromBuilding accepts filter===null and builds a route with cargo: []). Net effect: in REMOTE mode (the DEFAULT boot mode) creating an any-cargo route — the common case — ALWAYS fails, while LOCAL works. This is the same drop-on-the-wire class the prior audit hunted (place-building cargoLabel etc.), but here the field is forwarded yet rejected by an over-strict guard that doesn't account for the client sending null instead of omitting the field.
confirmed.evidence: Client REMOTE send: src/mutation-gateway.ts:643-645 `createRoute(...) { return send('create-route', { fromIslandId, toIslandId, buildingId, filterResource }); }`. Caller src/routes-ui.ts:590-595 passes `isAny ? null : (cargoChoice as ResourceId)` as filterResource; isAny is true for the default option (`__any__`, appended first at routes-ui.ts:468-471, label 'any (priority)'). Gateway interface src/mutation-gateway.ts:178-183 types filterResource as `ResourceId | null`. Server guard server/src/game/intents.ts:436-438 rejects null: `if (filterResource !== undefined && typeof filterResource !== 'string') return { ok: false, error: 'filterResource must be a string when present' };`. LOCAL ground truth: routes-ui.ts:598-602 and mutation-gateway.ts:444-450 call createRouteFromBuilding(building, from, to, isAny ? null : ..., dist) which (src/routes.ts:1090) builds `cargo: filter !== null ? [{resourceId: filter}] : []` — null is a valid, supported value meaning 'no filter / priority list empty'.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Make the server accept null as the well-formed 'any cargo' sentinel, matching the client wire shape and the pure fn contract. Change the guard at server/src/game/intents.ts:436 to allow null, e.g. `if (filterResource !== undefined && filterResource !== null && typeof filterResource !== 'string')`, and normalize at line 458 to `const filter = (filterResource === undefined || filterResource === null) ? null : (filterResource as ResourceId);` (the latter already maps undefined→null but not the null it now rejects upstream). Equivalent alternative: have the client omit the field when any (send undefined), but fixing the server is correct since null is the declared interface type and the pure-layer sentinel.

confirmed.title: REMOTE server never runs the §15.2 global ticks — drones, routes, settlement vehicles, all orbital systems, island merging and sonar are frozen for the default player
confirmed.description: SPEC §15.2 defines the tick model as 'Per-island advance + global route/drone/vehicle/sat ticks' (marked L = implemented). The client ticker runs all of these inside the `if (!isRemote)` block in main.ts (tickDrones main.ts:2544, tickRoutes :2556, tickSatMovement/tickSweeperCleanup/tickDebris/tickScannerDiscovery/tickCommPackets/tickRepairDrones :2569-2574, tickSonarBuoys :2581, tickVehicles :2589, findNextMerge/performMerge :2489-2495, tickActiveBonus/tickTradeOffers :2474-2475). The authoritative server's only advance is `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now)` in runtime.catchUp (runtime.ts:43) / loadAndCatchUp. advanceWorldEconomy (src/economy-advance.ts) ONLY advances per-island economy plus lattice/shared-network groups — it calls none of the global tick functions (grep of server/src and economy-advance.ts for tickDrones/tickRoutes/tickVehicles/tickSat*/tickScanner*/tickDebris/findNextMerge/performMerge returns zero matches). Because REMOTE is the default boot mode and the client's local tick is gated off (`if (!isRemote)`), every time-driven multi-system mechanic is permanently frozen for the default player: a dispatched scout drone never moves, never reveals corridor cells, never returns (§11.3-§11.6 dead); a created route never dispatches or delivers cargo (§2.4/§6 inter-island logistics dead — deliverArrivals/dispatch live only in tickRoutes); a settlement ship/helicopter never arrives so a discovered island can never be settled by vehicle (§12 dead, and a deleted route is set draining=true but its prune only runs in tickRoutes so it becomes a permanent zombie in the player's route list); satellites never orbit, never run scanner discovery, debris/Kessler never ticks, sweepers/repair-drones/comm-packets never resolve (§14 dead); ellipse-overlapping islands never merge (§3.6 dead); sonar buoys never reveal depth (§5 dead). These are not economy values — they are entirely separate stateful systems the migration left without a server driver. The intents that CREATE these entities (dispatch-drone, create-route, dispatch-settler, launch-satellite, etc.) succeed and persist the entity into world.drones/routes/vehicles/satellites, so the player sees the action 'work' then watches it hang forever. Appendix C documents only `advanceWorldEconomy` for catch-up and never acknowledges the missing global ticks, so §15.2 and the code are out of sync.
confirmed.evidence: runtime.ts:43 `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now);` is the sole advance. `grep -rn 'tickDrones|tickRoutes|tickVehicles|tickSatMovement|tickScannerDiscovery|tickDebris|findNextMerge|performMerge|tickSonarBuoys' server/src` → no matches; same grep over src/economy-advance.ts → no matches. Client runs them all behind `if (!isRemote)` (main.ts:2444 opens the block; tickDrones :2544, tickRoutes :2556, orbital ticks :2569-2574, tickVehicles :2589). SPEC.md:70 `| §15.2 Tick model | L | Per-island advance + global route/drone/vehicle/sat ticks. |`. tick fns are time-driven (tickDrones(world, nowMs, prevTickMs) drones.ts:679; tickRoutes(world, states, nowMs, elapsedSec, wallOffsetMs) routes.ts:805) so they are exactly what catch-up should invoke. delete-route zombie: intents.ts:649 sets `route.draining = true` but the only prune is routes.ts:824 inside tickRoutes which never runs server-side.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Run the global ticks in the authoritative catch-up the same way the client does. In runtime.catchUp, after advanceWorldEconomy, invoke the pure time-driven global ticks for the elapsed gap — tickRoutes, tickDrones, tickVehicles, the orbital tick chores (tickSatMovement/tickSweeperCleanup/tickDebris/tickScannerDiscovery/tickCommPackets/tickRepairDrones), tickSonarBuoys, and the findNextMerge/performMerge pass — ideally by extracting the client's whole post-economy tick block into a shared pure helper (mirroring how advanceWorldEconomy was extracted) so server and client cannot drift. Note these are wall-clock driven so a single catch-up call over the gap advances them coherently. Then update SPEC §15.2 / Appendix C to state the server runs the global ticks too, or, if some are intentionally deferred, document precisely which mechanics are disabled in REMOTE.

confirmed.title: Authoritative server never advances transport/exploration/orbital/merge systems — in-flight drones, routes, settlement vehicles, satellites, repair drones, debris, scanner discovery, comm packets, sonar buoys, and island merges are frozen in REMOTE (the default mode)
confirmed.description: The authoritative server's only world-advance path is `catchUp` → `advanceWorldEconomy` (runtime.ts:43; also the WS periodic push via ws.ts:85 and routes.ts:79). `advanceWorldEconomy` (src/economy-advance.ts) integrates ONLY the per-island production economy — it imports nothing from drones/settlement/orbital and calls none of `tickDrones`, `tickRoutes`, `tickVehicles`, `tickSatMovement`, `tickSweeperCleanup`, `tickDebris`, `tickScannerDiscovery`, `tickCommPackets`, `tickRepairDrones`, `tickSonarBuoys`, or `findNextMerge`/`performMerge` (its sole transport reference is the READ-ONLY `computeCableNetworkBalance` for the brownout factor). On the client, every one of those ticks lives inside the `if (!isRemote)` block in main.ts's ticker (main.ts:2444–2618), and the offline drone catch-up (`tickDrones`) at main.ts:2021 is gated on `restored`, which is only assigned in the LOCAL branch (main.ts:330) and stays null in REMOTE (main.ts:312–328). So in REMOTE — the DEFAULT boot mode — neither side ever advances these systems. Because all of this state IS serialized and round-tripped (persistence.ts:787 routes, :808 vehicles, :813 satellites, :815 repairDrones, :817 debrisFields, :832 commPackets, plus drones), the data persists but is permanently frozen. Concrete failure paths: a launched scout drone never moves, never reveals corridor cells, never discovers islands, and never returns its fuel; a created route never moves a single unit of cargo between islands; a dispatched settlement vehicle never arrives — meaning a REMOTE player can never settle a newly-discovered island, hard-blocking §12 progression and the entire mid/late game; launched satellites never move, scan, decay, or generate/clear debris (§14); repair drones never arrive; expanded islands never merge (§3.6). The SPEC mandates offline resolution for exactly these systems (§15.5 line 281 'Vehicles in flight while the player is offline are resolved at return-time using the deterministic forecast'; §11 'Drones travel in real time'), and the client even has dedicated offline drone catch-up — but the authoritative server implements none of it. This is the systemic cross-layer gap the prior by-area audit structurally missed: it caught only the narrow trade subset (migration-findings.json #241), not that all real-time world progression is dead server-side.
confirmed.evidence: src/economy-advance.ts:42 — the ONLY transport import is `import { computeCableNetworkBalance } from './routes.js';` (read-only brownout balance); grep for tickDrones|tickRoutes|tickVehicles|tickSat|findNextMerge across server/ returns nothing. server/src/game/runtime.ts:43 `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now);` is the entire catch-up body. src/main.ts:2444 `if (!isRemote) {` opens the block containing tickDrones (2544), tickRoutes (2556), tickSatMovement (2569), tickSweeperCleanup (2570), tickDebris (2571), tickScannerDiscovery (2572), tickCommPackets (2573), tickRepairDrones (2574), tickSonarBuoys (2581), tickVehicles (2589), findNextMerge/performMerge (2489–2495); block closes at 2618. src/main.ts:2021 `if (restored) {` gates offline tickDrones; restored only set at main.ts:330 (LOCAL else-branch), null in REMOTE. SPEC Appendix C (lines 2196–2227) documents the server advance as `advanceWorldEconomy` ONCE and lists only economy components — transport/orbital/merge ticks are absent from the documented server scope.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a server-side world-progression pass that runs the deterministic real-time ticks during catch-up and the periodic push, mirroring main.ts's non-render ticks. Extract a pure `advanceWorldSystems(world, islandStates, now, prevNow, weatherWallOffsetMs)` that sequences tickDrones, tickRoutes, tickVehicles, the orbital chores (tickSatMovement → tickSweeperCleanup → tickDebris → tickScannerDiscovery → tickCommPackets → tickRepairDrones), tickSonarBuoys, and the findNextMerge/performMerge loop in the same order main.ts uses, ensuring none of those modules import pixi.js (split render helpers out where needed, as island/world already do). Call it from `catchUp` after `advanceWorldEconomy`, threading the offline window (prev = min island lastTick → now). Update SPEC Appendix C to document the world-systems advance as part of the authoritative server scope. Until implemented, REMOTE cannot deliver §11/§12/§14 gameplay.

confirmed.title: REMOTE authoritative server never runs world-system ticks (drones/routes/vehicles/satellites/merge) — scouting, transport, settlement, orbital all dead in the default mode
confirmed.description: The server's only state-advance call is `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now)` inside `catchUp`/`loadAndCatchUp` (runtime.ts:43, 60-67). That shared pure module advances ONLY the per-island economy (economy.ts integration + NC/lattice/cable/solar/toxicity). It does NOT tick any world-level system. In the client these run in the ticker but are ALL gated behind `if (!isRemote)` (main.ts:2444): `tickDrones` (2544), `tickRoutes` (2556), `tickSatMovement`/`tickSweeperCleanup`/`tickDebris`/`tickScannerDiscovery`/`tickCommPackets`/`tickRepairDrones` (2569-2574), `tickSonarBuoys` (2581), `tickVehicles` (2589), and island merge `findNextMerge`/`performMerge` (2489-2495). A repo-wide grep confirms NONE of these are referenced anywhere under server/src/ (only a comment mention in intents.ts:641). Concrete failure paths in the new default REMOTE mode, where the server owns authoritative state: (1) a drone dispatched via the `dispatch-drone` intent is appended to `world.drones` with an `expectedReturnTime` but `tickDrones` (the return/discovery/loss processor) never runs, so the drone never returns and SCOUT DISCOVERY of new islands never happens; (2) `create-route` pushes a route but `tickRoutes`/`deliverArrivals` never run, so inter-island cargo is NEVER dispatched or delivered (§2.4 transport is inert); (3) `dispatch-settler` calls `dispatchVehicle` which pushes a vehicle (settlement.ts:528) but the arrival processor `tickVehicles` — which sets `target.populated = true` (settlement.ts:555) — never runs, so SETTLEMENT never completes and no new colony is ever created; (4) launched satellites never move/scan/clean debris; (5) overlapping expanded islands never merge. These mechanics only appear to work in casual testing because the LOCAL fallback ticks them. The server runtime tests (runtime.test.ts) assert only that island inventory grows, never that a dispatched drone returns / a route delivers / a settler arrives, so the green suite misses it.
confirmed.evidence: runtime.ts:43 `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now);` is the sole advance. economy-advance.ts has zero drone/route/vehicle/satellite references (it only loops island economy). main.ts:2444 `if (!isRemote) {` wraps tickDrones (2544), tickRoutes (2556), tickSatMovement..tickRepairDrones (2569-2574), tickVehicles (2589), findNextMerge (2489). `grep -rn 'tickRoutes|tickDrones|tickVehicles|tickScannerDiscovery|findNextMerge|deliverArrivals' server/src/` → only intents.ts:641 (a comment). settlement.ts:528 `world.vehicles.push(vehicle)`; settlement.ts:555 `target.populated = true` lives inside tickVehicles, never called server-side.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Extract the client ticker's world-system tick block into a shared pure orchestrator (e.g. `advanceWorld` calling tickDrones/tickRoutes/tickVehicles/orbital ticks/findNextMerge, mirroring how advanceWorldEconomy was extracted) and call it from `catchUp` immediately after advanceWorldEconomy, so the authoritative offline/online catch-up processes drone returns, route deliveries, settler arrivals, orbital, and merges exactly as LOCAL does. The ticks need the perf-clock `now`; pass it through (the integrator is already cadence-agnostic). Add server tests asserting a dispatched drone returns + discovers, a route delivers cargo, and a dispatched settler populates the target after catch-up. Update SPEC §15.6/Appendix C to state the authoritative advance must run the world-system ticks, not only the per-island economy.

confirmed.title: Server catch-up advances the economy only — drones, routes, settler vehicles, and repair drones are NEVER ticked, so exploration/transport/settlement are dead in REMOTE (the default mode)
confirmed.description: The server's authoritative catch-up (catchUp / loadAndCatchUp in server/src/game/runtime.ts) calls ONLY advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now). advanceWorldEconomy (src/economy-advance.ts) integrates the per-island ECONOMY (production/power/lattice/cables/solar/toxicity) and nothing else — it imports none of drones/settlement/routes. The mobile-entity simulation that the LOCAL client runs every frame — tickDrones, tickRoutes, tickVehicles, tickRepairDrones (src/main.ts:2544/2556/2574/2589) — is gated entirely inside the `if (!isRemote)` block (main.ts:2444), so in REMOTE the client does NOT run them either. There is no server-side game loop or interval that ticks them (the only server timer is the WS state-push, which calls catchUp). Net effect in REMOTE (the default boot mode): a dispatch-drone intent succeeds and appends a Drone to world.drones, but the drone never moves, never reaches its scan radius, and never flips isl.discovered / drains world.revealedCells (drones.ts advanceDrones is the sole driver of island discovery and fog reveal) — so the entire §11 exploration/discovery loop is frozen. A dispatch-settler intent appends a Vehicle but tickVehicles (settlement.ts:652, which sets target.populated = true on arrival, settlement.ts:555/729) never runs, so ship/helicopter settlement never completes. A create-route intent pushes a Route but tickRoutes (routes.ts:805) never moves cargo, so §2.4 inter-island transport moves zero resources. Repair drones and satellite in-orbit moves are likewise frozen. The prior audit fixed the economy-ctx divergence by routing the server through the shared advanceWorldEconomy, but that shared module only covers the economy; it structurally missed that catch-up must also drive the transport/mobile-entity ticks the client formerly ran in its ticker. Drones/routes/vehicles ARE serialized and pushed (persistence.ts:787-808; applyRemoteSnapshot Object.assigns them), so they appear in the UI — they just never advance, masking the freeze as 'stuck' rather than 'missing'.
confirmed.evidence: runtime.ts:43 `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now);` is the ONLY simulation call in catchUp. `grep -rn 'tickDrones|tickRoutes|tickVehicles|tickRepairDrones' server/src/` → no matches (only a comment in intents.ts:641). economy-advance.ts imports neither drones nor settlement nor tickRoutes (grep returns nothing). Client ticks are all inside `if (!isRemote)` opened at main.ts:2444: `tickDrones` (main.ts:2544), `tickRoutes` (2556), `tickRepairDrones` (2574), `tickVehicles` (2589). Discovery driver: drones.ts advanceDrones sets `isl.discovered = true`; settler arrival: settlement.ts:555 `target.populated = true`.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Extend the server catch-up to drive the full mobile-entity simulation, not just the economy: after advanceWorldEconomy, run the same tickDrones → discovery-flush → tickRoutes → tickRepairDrones → tickVehicles sequence the client runs in its ticker (covering the integration window from each entity's last tick to `now`, with the weather wall offset), ideally by extracting that block from main.ts into a shared pure helper (mirroring how advanceWorldEconomy was extracted) so client and server can't drift. Add server runtime tests asserting (a) a dispatched scout drone discovers an island after enough catch-up, (b) a dispatched settler populates its target, (c) a route moves cargo between islands.

confirmed.title: No Origin validation on the WS upgrade — Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking grants full authoritative mutation + state read with the victim's cookie
confirmed.description: The `/api/game/ws` upgrade's `preValidation` hook authenticates ONLY via the `ri_session` cookie (`resolveSession`) and never inspects the `Origin` header. The session cookie is set `sameSite: 'lax'` (server/src/auth/cookie.ts:11). SameSite=Lax does NOT protect WebSocket handshakes: a browser attaches the cookie to a cross-origin `new WebSocket('wss://islands.nitjsefni.eu/api/game/ws')` initiated from any malicious page the victim visits. The handshake therefore succeeds with the victim's session, and the attacker's page can then send arbitrary intent frames (place-building, dispatch-drone, settle, every wired intent in intents.ts) AND receives the full authoritative `{type:'state',snapshot}` push (info disclosure of the player's entire game). The nginx deploy block (server/deploy/README.md:24-30) proxies the Upgrade but adds no Origin gate either. This is the classic CSWSH vector and it is unmitigated end-to-end. The WS path is the ONLY mutation channel in REMOTE (the default mode), so this is full account takeover of game state via a drive-by page. The prior audit's WS work (frame-size cap, rate limit) did not address Origin/CSWSH; no test asserts an Origin rejection (ws.test.ts only tests the no-cookie 401 case).
confirmed.evidence: ws.ts:120-124 preValidation: `const user = await resolveSession(pool, req); if (!user) { await reply.code(401)...; return; } req.user = user;` — no `req.headers.origin` check. cookie.ts:11 `sameSite: 'lax'`. resolveSession (auth/guard.ts:19-23) reads only `req.cookies[SESSION_COOKIE]`. No `verifyClient`/origin logic anywhere (grep for origin/Origin in server/src returns only unrelated hits). nginx (deploy/README.md:24-30) sets Upgrade/Connection headers, no Origin enforcement.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Reject the upgrade in `preValidation` when `req.headers.origin` is absent or not in an allowlist of trusted origins (e.g. `https://islands.nitjsefni.eu`, plus the dev origin in non-prod). Browsers always send Origin on WS handshakes, so a strict same-origin check closes CSWSH without affecting native clients. Optionally also raise the session cookie to `sameSite: 'strict'` for the HTTP POST mutation routes. Add a ws.test.ts case asserting a foreign Origin is rejected with 401/403.

confirmed.title: dispatch-settler deducts fuel+foundation_kits but the vehicle NEVER arrives server-side (tickVehicles is never run) — permanent resource loss, island never settles
confirmed.description: The dispatch-settler intent (intents.ts:845) calls dispatchVehicle, which immediately deducts the loaded fuel and foundation_kit(s) from the origin island's inventory (settlement.ts:506-508) and pushes a SettlementVehicle with status:'active' onto world.vehicles (settlement.ts:528). The ONLY code path that completes an in-flight vehicle — flipping target.populated, placing the dock, building the colony's IslandState (populateSettledIsland, settlement.ts:739) — is tickVehicles (settlement.ts:652). On the server, the sole state-advance path is advanceWorldEconomy in src/economy-advance.ts (called from runtime.ts catchUp/loadAndCatchUp), which runs ONLY the per-island economy integrator + terrain-shot resolution. It never calls tickVehicles (confirmed: grep for tickVehicles across server/ returns only a comment at intents.ts:641). The vehicle is persisted (persistence.ts:808 `vehicles: [...world.vehicles]`), so it survives every catch-up with status:'active', expectedArrivalTime in the past, and is never processed. A REMOTE player (REMOTE is the default boot mode) thus pays the full fuel+kit cost, the intent returns ok:true, but the target island is NEVER populated and the resources are unrecoverable. This is distinct from the already-fixed 'advanceIsland runs with no ctx' economy-divergence finding and from any 'visuals frozen' finding — it is real authoritative resource destruction with no completion path. Asymmetry: settle-via-spacetime (intents.ts:879) calls settleViaSpacetimeAnchor which invokes populateSettledIsland synchronously (instant, no tick), so the T5 instant-settle path WORKS while the ordinary ship/helicopter dispatch is permanently broken.
confirmed.evidence: intents.ts:866 `const r = dispatchVehicle(game.world, origin.spec, origin.state, targetSpec, kind, tier, fuelLoaded, foundationKitCount, now);` → settlement.ts:506-508 deduct fuel+foundation_kit, settlement.ts:528 `world.vehicles.push(vehicle)` with status:'active'. Completion only via settlement.ts:739 `populateSettledIsland(...)` inside tickVehicles (settlement.ts:652). Server advance: runtime.ts:43 `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now)`; economy-advance.ts runs only advanceIsland/advanceLatticeGroup/advanceSharedNetworkGroup + resolveShot — no tickVehicles. `grep -rn tickVehicles server/` → only intents.ts:641 (a comment). Vehicle persisted: persistence.ts:808 `vehicles: [...world.vehicles]`, deserialized persistence.ts:1015.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Run the per-frame world ticks server-side as part of the authoritative advance. Add a pure tickWorld step (tickVehicles, tickSatMovement, tickSweeperCleanup, tickDebris, tickScannerDiscovery, tickCommPackets, tickRepairDrones, tickDrones, tickRoutes, tickSonarBuoys — mirroring main.ts:2544-2589) invoked inside advanceWorldEconomy (or a sibling called from catchUp/loadAndCatchUp) so dispatched entities reach their completion path on the server. These functions are all in the pure layer (settlement.ts, orbital.ts, drones.ts, routes.ts) and import no pixi.js, so they can be imported by the server. They must run on the SAME catch-up clock so a 30-day offline gap still resolves arrivals deterministically.

confirmed.title: launch-satellite deducts payload+orbital_insertion_package+antimatter but the satellite is stuck locked:false forever (tickSatMovement never runs server-side) — sat never becomes operational, cost lost
confirmed.description: launch-satellite (intents.ts:902) calls launchSatellite, which deducts the variant payload resource + orbital_insertion_package + antimatter_propellant (orbital.ts:282-284) and, on a successful launch roll, pushes a Satellite spawned with locked:false and a movingTo plan (orbital.ts:344-345, 358). Per the in-code contract, the launch IS the first move: the sat ferries itself to the target and tickSatMovement flips locked back to true on arrival (orbital.ts:341-344). tickSatMovement (orbital.ts:722) is never called server-side (grep across server/ confirms zero call sites). Because advanceWorldEconomy only runs the economy integrator, the satellite is persisted (persistence.ts:813) and survives catch-ups permanently as locked:false / in-transit. A locked:false sat provides no scanner coverage, no relay/comm, no mirror solar boost, and cannot be repaired or re-moved — i.e. the player paid the (expensive, T6) payload+antimatter cost for an entity that NEVER does anything. The successful intent returns ok:true and deducts the cost regardless. This is reachable by any REMOTE player with a spaceport + ascendant core, and the loss is unrecoverable.
confirmed.evidence: intents.ts:910 `const r = launchSatellite(game.world, islandId, variant, targetX, targetY, now);` → orbital.ts:282-284 deduct needed resources, orbital.ts:344 `locked: false`, orbital.ts:345 `movingTo: { x: targetX, y: targetY, arrivalMs: nowMs + travelSec * 1000 }`, orbital.ts:358 `world.satellites.push(sat)`. Lock-on-arrival only in tickSatMovement (orbital.ts:722) — comment at orbital.ts:343 `tickSatMovement flips locked back to true on arrival`. No server call site: `grep -rn tickSatMovement server/` → none. Server advance path (runtime.ts:43 → economy-advance.ts) never ticks orbital. Satellites persisted: persistence.ts:813 `satellites: [...world.satellites]`.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Same root fix as the dispatch-settler finding: invoke the orbital ticks (tickSatMovement, tickSweeperCleanup, tickDebris, tickScannerDiscovery, tickCommPackets, tickRepairDrones — the main.ts:2569-2574 ordering) inside the server's authoritative advance, on the catch-up clock. tickSatMovement will then flip locked:true on arrival so launched satellites become operational server-side.

confirmed.title: Artificial-island construction bypasses the mutation-gateway entirely — building an island is a client-local no-op reverted by the next ~1s authoritative snapshot in REMOTE
confirmed.description:
The §13 land-reclamation 'construct artificial island' mechanic never reaches the server. In REMOTE (the DEFAULT boot mode) this means: building an artificial island appears to succeed for up to ~1s, then the next authoritative server snapshot push wipes the new island, restores the founder's debited materials, and the island never persists server-side — it simply vanishes. This is the third and largest instance of the gateway-bypass class the prior audit caught (convert-to-servitor, biome-reassign were inspector toggles; this is a whole island-creation mechanic).

Failure path:
1. construction-ui.ts:604 calls `constructIsland(world.seed, state, spec, req, {cx,cy}, id, performance.now(), displayName)` directly — NOT through the mutation-gateway. `state` and `spec` are the live client-local objects from `options.islandStates.get(selectedFounder)` / `options.world.islands.find(...)`.
2. `constructIsland` (src/artificial-island.ts:182-187) mutates `founderState.inventory[r] -= n` (debits materials on the local object) and mints `newSpec`/`newState` (lines 195-209).
3. construction-ui.ts:614-619 hands the result to `onConstruct`, which in main.ts:1609-1619 pushes the new island into client-LOCAL state ONLY: `worldState.islands.push(newSpec)`, `islandStates.set(newSpec.id, newState)`, `islandSpecsById.set(...)`, `modifierMulsById.set(...)`, `rebuildWorldLayers()`. No WS intent is ever sent.
4. There is NO 'construct-island' intent in the server INTENTS catalog (intents.ts:180-602 — keys are place-building, demolish-building, cancel-construction, upgrade-building, set-active-floors, dispatch-drone, create-route, unlock-skill-node, relocate-building, set-force-run, relabel-cargo, expand-island, cancel-queued-upgrade, delete-route, set-route-mode, set-cargo-weight, set-cargo-floor-pct, reorder-route-cargo, set-route-cargo, buy-keystone, bind-crystal, unbind-crystal, tier-reset, dispatch-settler, settle-via-spacetime, launch-satellite, upgrade-spaceport, move-satellite, dispatch-repair-drone — no construct-island).
5. There is NO constructIsland method on the mutation-gateway (src/mutation-gateway.ts has only cancelConstruction at lines 126/272/566). Contrast the sister reclamation action expand-island, which DOES have both a gateway method (mutation-gateway.ts:579 `send('expand-island', ...)`) and a server intent (intents.ts:587-602). Construction has neither.
6. The next server state push runs applyRemoteSnapshot (main.ts:2098), which `Object.assign(worldState, d.world)` overwriting `worldState.islands` with the server's list (the local spec is gone), then `islandSpecsById.clear()` + rebuild from `worldState.islands` only (main.ts:2140-2145), and `islandStates.delete(id)` for any key the server doesn't report (main.ts:2122-2124). The new island spec+state are dropped and the founder inventory debit is reverted. Net: a visually-flickering no-op; the player can never build an artificial island while server-authoritative.
confirmed.evidence: construction-ui.ts:604-619 calls constructIsland(...) + onConstruct({newSpec,newState,founderId,nowMs}) with NO gateway. main.ts:1609-1619 onConstruct pushes directly: `worldState.islands.push(newSpec); islandStates.set(newSpec.id, newState); islandSpecsById.set(newSpec.id, newSpec);`. server/src/game/intents.ts INTENTS catalog (180-602) has no 'construct-island' key. mutation-gateway.ts exposes only cancelConstruction (126/272/566), never constructIsland. applyRemoteSnapshot main.ts:2115 `Object.assign(worldState, d.world)` + 2140 `islandSpecsById.clear()` + 2122-2124 delete-missing islandStates reverts the local-only island within ~1s.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a 'construct-island' intent to server/src/game/intents.ts that re-runs the pure validateConstruction + positionIsFree + constructIsland against authoritative server state (mirroring expand-island's authoritative-gate pattern: resolve founder island, gate on validateConstruction, debit materials, mint+insert the new island into the server world), with the server minting the artificial id and timestamps server-side. Add a constructIsland method to the mutation-gateway interface + both impls (LOCAL: call the pure constructIsland directly as today; REMOTE: send('construct-island', {founderId, biome, majorRadius, minorRadius, cx, cy, displayName})). Route construction-ui.tryConstruct through the gateway instead of calling constructIsland + onConstruct directly. As a secondary fix, stop stamping perf-domain performance.now() into island timestamps — server intents derive timestamps from wall-clock; in REMOTE the server should mint nowMs from its own clock (currently moot because the island never reaches the server, but it would produce a bad lastTick if naively forwarded).

confirmed.title: Orbital satellite/repair/debris lifecycle ticks never run server-side — launched sats are permanently 'in transit', non-functional, and un-relocatable in REMOTE
confirmed.description:
In REMOTE mode (the new default) the server is authoritative and the client's per-frame world ticks are gated behind `if (!isRemote)` (src/main.ts:2444 opens the block; the orbital chores at src/main.ts:2569-2574 — tickSatMovement / tickSweeperCleanup / tickDebris / tickScannerDiscovery / tickCommPackets / tickRepairDrones — live inside it and never execute in REMOTE). The server-side catch-up (`catchUp` / `loadAndCatchUp`, runtime.ts:24-67) runs ONLY `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now)` (runtime.ts:43). advanceWorldEconomy contains no orbital lifecycle ticks (src/economy-advance.ts imports only `effectiveSolarBoostFor` from orbital.ts — a read, not a tick). grep across server/src confirms ZERO calls to tickSatMovement/tickRepairDrones/tickDebris/tickSweeperCleanup/tickScannerDiscovery/tickCommPackets anywhere on the server.

The `launch-satellite` intent IS implemented server-side (server/src/game/intents.ts:902-913 → orbital.ts launchSatellite) and is reachable by any REMOTE player with a Spaceport + Ascendant Core. launchSatellite (orbital.ts:331-358) appends a satellite with `locked: false` and `movingTo: { x, y, arrivalMs: nowMs + travelSec*1000 }`. The ONLY code that flips `locked` back to true and clears `movingTo` on arrival is tickSatMovement (orbital.ts:757-767) — which never runs server-side. So in REMOTE every launched satellite is stuck `locked:false`/`movingTo`-set forever.

This is fatal because EVERY functional satellite path gates on `sat.locked`: comm graph skips `if (!sat.locked) continue` (orbital.ts:417, 543 — 'in-transit satellites are unreachable'); scanner discovery skips `!sat.locked` (orbital.ts:824, 1140); mirror solar boost `effectiveSolarBoostFor` skips `!sat.locked`; sweeper cleanup counts only locked sats (orbital.ts:874+). So a launched sat does nothing: no scanner fog-clear, no Mirror-Sat solar boost, no relay comm range, no debris sweeping. Worse, `requestSatMove` (orbital.ts:704-706) rejects with `not-locked`/`already-moving`, so the player can never redirect or recover the dead sat. dispatch-repair-drone is also implemented server-side (intents.ts:946-958) but tickRepairDrones (orbital.ts:639-680, the only code that removes the drone and clears `pendingRepairDroneId`) never runs — so a dispatched repair drone never completes and `pendingRepairDroneId` stays set forever, permanently blocking further repair/move on that sat.

The orbital-ui roster surfaces this as a permanent symptom: orbital-ui.ts:562 renders status as `sat.locked ? 'locked' : sat.movingTo ? 'in transit' : 'free'` → permanently 'in transit'; computeBodySig (orbital-ui.ts:610) encodes a permanent 'M'. satellite-overlay.ts:113 keeps the repair indicator drawn while `pendingRepairDroneId !== null` → permanent repair glyph. The sat overlay also draws the sat at its static launch position (satellite-overlay.ts:76-77 reads sat.x/sat.y, no interpolation), so a 'moving' sat visibly never leaves the Spaceport. This is REMOTE-only; LOCAL runs all six ticks every frame so the lifecycle completes.

Note on the probe hypothesis: orbital-ui does NOT compute a perf-vs-epoch countdown (it reads only the static locked/movingTo flags, no `performance.now() - arrivalMs` delta), so the 'garbage countdown' framing is not the defect. The real, reachable defect is the missing server-side lifecycle ticks, which the orbital panel surfaces as the permanently-'in transit' / permanently-non-functional satellite described above. deserializeWorld's perfShift (persistence.ts:1021-1030) correctly remaps sat/repair-drone timestamps on load, but that remap is moot when the ticks that consume those timestamps never fire server-side.
confirmed.evidence: runtime.ts:43 `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now);` — the ONLY world advance in catchUp; no orbital ticks. economy-advance.ts: only `import { effectiveSolarBoostFor } from './orbital.js'` (no tick imports). main.ts:2444 `if (!isRemote) {` wraps main.ts:2569 `tickSatMovement(worldState, now);` … main.ts:2574 `tickRepairDrones(worldState, now);` (block closes main.ts:2618). orbital.ts:344-345 launch sets `locked: false, movingTo: {…, arrivalMs: nowMs + travelSec*1000}`. orbital.ts:757-767 (tickSatMovement) is the sole `sat.locked = true; sat.movingTo = undefined;` site. Functional gates: orbital.ts:417 `if (!sat.locked) continue;`, :543 `if (!sat.locked) continue; // in-transit satellites are unreachable`, :824 + :1140 scanner `if (!sat.locked)`, effectiveSolarBoostFor `if (!sat.locked) continue;`. orbital.ts:704-706 requestSatMove rejects `already-moving`/`not-locked`. intents.ts:902-913 launch-satellite + :933 move-satellite + :946-958 dispatch-repair-drone all server-implemented and reachable. `grep -rn 'tickSat|tickRepair|tickDebris|tickSweeper|tickScanner|tickComm' server/src` → no matches. Prior audit (docs/reports/migration-findings.json) covers runtime ctx-drift and stale panel references but has NO finding for the missing orbital lifecycle ticks.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Run the orbital lifecycle ticks server-side in the same catch-up integration that runs advanceWorldEconomy. In runtime.ts catchUp, after world.islandStates is set and the economy advance runs, call the time-driven world ticks against the deserialized `now` clock: tickSatMovement(world, now), tickSweeperCleanup(world, deltaMs), tickDebris(world, now, deltaMs), tickScannerDiscovery(world, deltaMs, now), tickCommPackets(world), tickRepairDrones(world, now) — mirroring main.ts:2569-2574, with deltaMs = real elapsed offline gap (deserializeWorld already collapses lastTick to that gap). Because these ticks process all events whose arrivalMs ≤ now in one pass (orbital.ts:730 `if (nowMs < sat.movingTo.arrivalMs)` guard), a single catch-up call resolves arrivals correctly for both a 1s and a 30d gap, same property advanceIsland relies on. Apply the identical fix to tickDrones/tickRoutes/tickVehicles/tickSonarBuoys, which share this exact `!isRemote`-gated, never-run-server-side root cause (in-flight drones never return, route cargo never lands, settlement vehicles never arrive in REMOTE). Add a server test: launch a satellite, catch up past arrivalMs, assert sat.locked===true && sat.movingTo===undefined and that scanner coverage/comm range take effect.

confirmed.title: Server unlock-skill-node uses DEFAULT_GRAPH not effectiveGraph — crystal mini-tree nodes are unpurchasable on REMOTE (the default mode)
confirmed.description:
The LOCAL gateway purchases skill nodes against the per-island effective graph: src/mutation-gateway.ts:384-386 does `const graph = effectiveGraph(island.state); buyNode(graph, island.state, nodeId)`. The server's authoritative `unlock-skill-node` handler instead uses the static `DEFAULT_GRAPH` for BOTH the pre-check and the buy: `nodePurchaseStatus(DEFAULT_GRAPH, state, nodeId)` then `buyNode(DEFAULT_GRAPH, state, nodeId)` (intents.ts:504,506).

`effectiveGraph(state)` (src/skilltree.ts:622-686) returns DEFAULT_GRAPH only when the island has NO socket bindings; once a crystal is bound it appends the synthetic socket node plus all crystal mini-tree nodes (id `${socketId}.${crystalId}.${suffix}`) and their edges to graph.nodes/edges. Those mini-tree nodes are real purchasable §9.3 skill nodes — the graft mechanic's whole point.

Because the server checks against DEFAULT_GRAPH, a mini-tree node is absent from `graph.nodes`, so `nodePurchaseStatus` hits the `const node = graph.nodes.find(...)` miss at skilltree.ts:1795-1796 and returns `'unreachable'`. The handler's `if (status !== 'purchasable') return { ok:false, error:status }` (intents.ts:505) then rejects EVERY crystal mini-tree purchase. REMOTE is the default boot mode (AGENTS.md), so for all normal players the crystal-graft skill progression is silently dead: src/skilltree-graphview.ts:371-372 calls `gateway.unlockSkillNode`, gets `!res.ok`, and returns without refresh — the node never buys and no error surfaces. It works fine on LOCAL (effectiveGraph path), so the bug is invisible in client-only debug runs.
confirmed.evidence: intents.ts:504 `const status = nodePurchaseStatus(DEFAULT_GRAPH, state, nodeId);` and :506 `buyNode(DEFAULT_GRAPH, state, nodeId);` vs mutation-gateway.ts:384 `const graph = effectiveGraph(island.state);` :386 `buyNode(graph, island.state, nodeId);`. skilltree.ts:1795-1796: `const node = graph.nodes.find((n) => n.id === target); if (!node) return 'unreachable';` — mini-tree node id is not in DEFAULT_GRAPH.nodes. effectiveGraph (skilltree.ts:635-666) injects nodes id=`${socketId}.${crystalId}.${nodeDef.idSuffix}` only when socketBindings is non-empty.
confirmed.suggestedFix: In the unlock-skill-node handler, build the per-island graph the same way LOCAL does and use it for both the pre-check and the buy: `const graph = effectiveGraph(state); const status = nodePurchaseStatus(graph, state, nodeId); ... buyNode(graph, state, nodeId);`. Import effectiveGraph into server/src/game/intents.ts. (The keystone pre-reject at intents.ts:498-500 stays as-is; keystones aren't in the crystal mini-tree.) Add a server intent test: bind a crystal, then unlock-skill-node a mini-tree node succeeds.

confirmed.title: Server never runs tickDrones/tickRoutes — in-flight drones and route batches NEVER arrive; the frozen-transport state is permanently committed on the next intent
confirmed.description: The server's ONLY world-advance path is advanceWorldEconomy (called from catchUp at runtime.ts:43, used by both the read-only WS push in ws.ts:85 and the persisting loadAndCatchUp at runtime.ts:62). advanceWorldEconomy integrates economy (inventories/XP/wear/power/lattice/cables/toxicity) but does NOT process transport: it never calls tickDrones (drones.ts:679) or tickRoutes/deliverArrivals (routes.ts:805,690). The client, by contrast, ALWAYS ticks transport — a one-shot offline catch-up tickDrones at restore (src/main.ts:2021-2037) plus per-frame tickDrones AND tickRoutes (src/main.ts:2544-2556). Confirmed by grep: server src references tickRoutes only in a comment (server/src/game/intents.ts:641) and never imports tickDrones at all; intents.ts:34 imports only dispatchDrone (launch), routes.ts imports only route-creation helpers. Consequence for a REMOTE (default, authoritative) account: a launched drone's expectedReturnTime and a route batch's arrivalTime elapse, but no server pass ever delivers the mission — the drone stays in world.drones forever, the batch stays in route.inFlight forever, and its cargo/scan-discovery payload is NEVER credited to any island. The economy meanwhile advances 10 days as if those deliveries never happened. This is a permanent loss of all in-flight cargo and all offline-window scan discoveries on server accounts, plus unbounded growth of the drones[]/inFlight[] arrays (every dispatched drone leaks). NOTE: the sibling 'no-RatesContext catch-up' bug the prior audit found (migration-findings.json, confirmed[]) was about WRONG production math and is fixed by routing through advanceWorldEconomy; this transport-tick omission is a DISTINCT, unreported gap — advanceWorldEconomy fixed economy parity but silently has no transport-processing counterpart.
confirmed.evidence: runtime.ts:43 `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now);` is the sole advance call. economy-advance.ts:83-282 contains zero references to tickDrones/tickRoutes/deliverArrivals (advances economy only). `cd server && grep -rn 'tickDrones\|deliverArrivals' src/` returns nothing executable; `grep -n tickRoutes src/game/intents.ts` => only line 641 (a comment). Contrast src/main.ts:2544 `const droneResult = tickDrones(worldState, now, prevFrameMs);` and :2556 `tickRoutes(worldState, islandStates, now, elapsedSec, weatherWallOffsetMs);` plus the restore-time offline catch-up at main.ts:2027 `const catchUp = tickDrones(worldState, catchUpNowMs, prevMs);`.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add transport processing to the shared server advance. The cleanest seam: inside catchUp (runtime.ts), after advanceWorldEconomy, run the offline transport catch-up exactly as the client does at restore — tickDrones(world, now, prevMs) and tickRoutes(world, islandStates, now, elapsedSec, wallOffset), where prevMs is the pre-advance min(lastTick) (the offline-window start) so the entire gap is processed in one pass (drones.ts/routes.ts integrators are gap-agnostic, matching main.ts:2010-2027's rationale). This must run on the PERSISTING path (loadAndCatchUp) so deliveries commit; mirror it on the read path so projections reflect arrivals. Add a server vitest: launch a drone, advance past expectedReturnTime via loadAndCatchUp, assert the drone left world.drones and its cargo/discovery landed — and that an in-flight route batch is delivered.

confirmed.title: Server place-building ocean validator gets TILE coords where it expects CELL indices (16× off) — authoritative ocean-placement validation checks the wrong location
confirmed.description:
The prior C20 fix (commit e2560bc) added ocean-def routing to the 'place-building' handler so REMOTE ocean platforms are no longer rejected with 'def-is-ocean'. That fix landed and the payload-drop for cargoLabel/anchorIslandId/terrainTarget/terrainShotMs is correctly forwarded (intents.ts:191,245-251 mirror the LOCAL gateway at mutation-gateway.ts:236-249, arg order/arity identical). BUT the new ocean-validation routing passes the wrong coordinate domain to validateOceanPlacement.

Ground truth (client + pure layer): ocean buildings store b.x/b.y as island-local TILE coords (economy.ts:681-684,706-708 lift via `anchor.cx + b.x`). The client validates with CELL indices: placement-ui.ts:676 `cellX = Math.floor(wt.x / CELL_SIZE_TILES)`, then `validateOceanPlacement(world, defId, cellX, cellY)` (placement-ui.ts:687). It then sends the gateway anchor-local TILE coords: `localX = cellAnchorCoordX - anchorSpec.cx` where `cellAnchorCoordX = cellX * CELL_SIZE_TILES` (placement-ui.ts:713,746).

Server: `x` received == that anchor-local TILE coord. It computes `x + spec.cx = cellAnchorCoordX = cellX * CELL_SIZE_TILES` (a world-TILE coord) and feeds it straight into `validateOceanPlacement(game.world, typedDefId, x + spec.cx, y + spec.cy)`. But validateOceanPlacement treats its 3rd/4th args as CELL indices and multiplies by CELL_SIZE_TILES internally (placement.ts: `tx0 = cx * CELL_SIZE_TILES`). So the server samples tiles at `cellX * 16 * 16 = cellX * 256` — 16× too far out (CELL_SIZE_TILES = 16, constants.ts:13). The handler comment at intents.ts:230-232 even mislabels the result as 'world-cell coords', conflating tile and cell domains.

Net: in REMOTE (the default boot mode) the authoritative server ocean-placement check evaluates terrain-match and ocean-vs-ocean-overlap at the wrong cell entirely. The land-overlap guard tends to pass spuriously (far-out open-ocean cells default to 'deep'/ocean), so illegitimate placements (terrain mismatch, overlapping another platform, or near land) can be wrongly ACCEPTED, defeating the server re-validation the migration exists to provide; conversely a legitimately-validated client placement is re-checked at a bogus location. This is REMOTE-only — the LOCAL path (placement-ui.ts:752 direct pure call) validated with the correct cellX/cellY before sending.
confirmed.evidence:
server/src/game/intents.ts:234 `const ov = validateOceanPlacement(game.world, typedDefId, x + spec.cx, y + spec.cy);` — x is island-local TILE, x+spec.cx is world-TILE.
placement.ts validateOceanPlacement body: `const tx0 = cx * CELL_SIZE_TILES;` — args are CELL indices.
placement-ui.ts:676 `const cellX = Math.floor(wt.x / CELL_SIZE_TILES);` then :687 `validateOceanPlacement(world, activeDefId, cellX, cellY)` (client validates with cell indices).
placement-ui.ts:713 `const cellAnchorCoordX = cellX * CELL_SIZE_TILES;` :746 `const localX = cellAnchorCoordX - anchorSpec.cx;` (sends anchor-local TILE coords).
constants.ts:13 `export const CELL_SIZE_TILES = 16;`
economy.ts:706-708 `// b.x, b.y are island-local on the anchor; lift to world tiles via ... isOceanTile(world, anchor.cx + b.x, anchor.cy + b.y)` (stored coords are tiles).
confirmed.suggestedFix: Convert the world-tile coord back to a CELL index before validating: `const ov = validateOceanPlacement(game.world, typedDefId, (x + spec.cx) / CELL_SIZE_TILES, (y + spec.cy) / CELL_SIZE_TILES);` (import CELL_SIZE_TILES from ../../../src/constants.js). For the legitimate client path `x + spec.cx === cellX * CELL_SIZE_TILES`, so integer division recovers cellX exactly. Also fix the misleading comment at intents.ts:230-232 ('world-cell coords' → tile vs cell). Add a server WS-path test that places an ocean platform near land / overlapping an existing platform and asserts the server REJECTS it, which the current code fails to do.

confirmed.title: unlock-skill-node server uses DEFAULT_GRAPH, not effectiveGraph — bound-crystal mini-tree nodes are unbuyable in REMOTE
confirmed.description:
The LOCAL mutation-gateway path computes the per-island effective skill graph and buys against it: src/mutation-gateway.ts:381-390 does `const graph = effectiveGraph(island.state); buyNode(graph, island.state, nodeId)`. effectiveGraph (src/skilltree.ts:622-686) unions DEFAULT_GRAPH with the mini-tree nodes/edges of every bound crystal, synthesizing node ids like `${socketId}.${crystalId}.${idSuffix}`. These crystal nodes exist ONLY in effectiveGraph, never in DEFAULT_GRAPH.

The client routes purchases of these very nodes through the same intent: skilltree-graphview.ts:369-372 builds `effectiveGraph(state)`, renders every node including bound-crystal mini-trees (drawNodes() line ~537 'catalog + bound crystal mini-trees'), and on click of any non-keystone node calls `deps.gateway.unlockSkillNode(state.id, nodeId)` → src/mutation-gateway.ts:627-628 `send('unlock-skill-node', { islandId, nodeId })`.

The server handler (server/src/game/intents.ts:486-508) instead validates and buys against DEFAULT_GRAPH: `nodePurchaseStatus(DEFAULT_GRAPH, state, nodeId)` (line 504) and `buyNode(DEFAULT_GRAPH, state, nodeId)` (line 506). For a crystal mini-tree node id, `nodePurchaseStatus` looks it up via `graph.nodes.find(n => n.id === target)` (skilltree.ts:1795); since the node is absent from DEFAULT_GRAPH it returns 'unreachable' (line 1796), so the handler rejects with error 'unreachable' at line 505 — the intent never reaches buyNode.

Result: after a player binds a crystal (server fully supports this via the 'bind-crystal' intent, intents.ts:781-804, which sets state.socketBindings — exercised in server/src/game/intents.test.ts:946,973), every attempt to buy that crystal's mini-tree nodes succeeds in LOCAL mode but is silently rejected in REMOTE (the default boot mode). The graphview swallows the rejection (`if (!res.ok) return;` at line 372), so the node simply never lights up — a player-visible feature (crystal grafting / mini-tree skill purchases, SPEC §9.3) is non-functional online.
confirmed.evidence:
LOCAL (src/mutation-gateway.ts:381-390): `const graph = effectiveGraph(island.state); buyNode(graph, island.state, nodeId)`.
REMOTE handler (server/src/game/intents.ts:504-506): `const status = nodePurchaseStatus(DEFAULT_GRAPH, state, nodeId); if (status !== 'purchasable') return { ok: false, error: status }; buyNode(DEFAULT_GRAPH, state, nodeId);`.
nodePurchaseStatus on a node absent from the graph (src/skilltree.ts:1795-1796): `const node = graph.nodes.find((n) => n.id === target); if (!node) return 'unreachable';`.
effectiveGraph synthesizes crystal node ids absent from DEFAULT_GRAPH (src/skilltree.ts:656-666).
confirmed.suggestedFix: Make the server resolve the same per-island effective graph the LOCAL path uses. In the 'unlock-skill-node' handler, import effectiveGraph and replace both DEFAULT_GRAPH references: `const graph = effectiveGraph(state); const status = nodePurchaseStatus(graph, state, nodeId); if (status !== 'purchasable') return { ok: false, error: status }; buyNode(graph, state, nodeId);`. The keystone pre-reject at lines 498-500 stays (keystones still excluded). This restores LOCAL/REMOTE parity for bound-crystal mini-tree purchases while keeping the authoritative SP/tier/reachability recompute on server state. Add a server intent test: bind a crystal, then unlock-skill-node a mini-tree node id, assert ok:true and SP charged.

confirmed.title: delete-route on REMOTE never removes a no-in-flight route — LOCAL hard-removes it, server only sets draining=true and never prunes
confirmed.description: Strict 4-layer trace of delete-route. LAYER 1 (REMOTE gateway, src/mutation-gateway.ts:646-648): deleteRoute(routeId) -> send('delete-route', { routeId }). LAYER 2 (LOCAL gateway = ground truth, src/mutation-gateway.ts:456-468): finds the route, and if route.inFlight.length === 0 it IMMEDIATELY removes it via world.routes.splice(idx,1); only when cargo is in flight does it set route.draining = true (soft-delete to drain). LAYER 3 (server handler, intents.ts:642-651): destructures { routeId }, validates it's a string, resolves the route, then UNCONDITIONALLY does route.draining = true and returns ok. It has NO immediate-removal branch — it never inspects inFlight.length and never splices the route out. LAYER 4 (prune): the server's only mechanism to actually delete a drained route is tickRoutes (src/routes.ts:822-825: 'if (r.draining && r.inFlight.length === 0) world.routes.splice(i,1)'), but tickRoutes is NEVER called server-side (see companion finding). Net effect on the default REMOTE mode: deleting a route that has no cargo in flight (the common case) does NOT remove it. The route persists in world.routes with draining=true, gets serialized by projectGame -> serializeWorld (persistence.ts:791 maps world.routes wholesale, including draining ones), and is pushed back to the client forever as a zombie route. The LOCAL path would have removed it instantly. This is exactly the contract-parity / behavior-divergence class the audit targets (LOCAL enforces a removal the server skips): the user clicks delete, the route visibly disappears under LOCAL but reappears/persists under REMOTE.
confirmed.evidence:
src/mutation-gateway.ts:456-466 (LOCAL, GROUND TRUTH): `deleteRoute(routeId) { const route = findRoute(routeId); if (!route) return err('route not found'); if (route.inFlight.length === 0) { const idx = world.routes.indexOf(route); if (idx >= 0) world.routes.splice(idx, 1); } else { route.draining = true; } return ok(); }`
src/mutation-gateway.ts:646-648 (REMOTE): `deleteRoute(routeId) { return send('delete-route', { routeId }); }`
server/src/game/intents.ts:642-651 (HANDLER): `'delete-route': { apply(game, payload) { if (!isRecord(payload)) ...; const { routeId } = payload; if (typeof routeId !== 'string') ...; const route = resolveRoute(game, routeId); if (!route) return { ok:false, error:'route not found' }; route.draining = true; return { ok:true }; } }` — no inFlight.length check, no splice.
src/routes.ts:822-825 (the prune the server depends on but never runs): `for (let i = world.routes.length-1; i>=0; i--) { const r = world.routes[i]; if (r && r.draining && r.inFlight.length === 0) world.routes.splice(i,1); }` inside tickRoutes, whose only call site is src/main.ts:2556.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Mirror the LOCAL ground truth in the handler: after resolveRoute, if route.inFlight.length === 0, splice it out of game.world.routes immediately; otherwise set route.draining = true. (This is correct and sufficient given that the server never dispatches cargo so inFlight is in practice always empty server-side.) Add a WS-path intent test: create a route, delete it, assert it is absent from the projected world.routes (not merely draining=true). Better still, also fix the companion tickRoutes gap so draining routes that DO have in-flight cargo eventually prune.

confirmed.title: tickRoutes is never invoked server-side — route delivery, dispatch, AND draining-route pruning are all inert in REMOTE
confirmed.description: Root cause underlying the delete-route divergence, but broader. The server's authoritative catch-up runs advanceWorldEconomy (server/src/game/runtime.ts:43) from the shared pure module src/economy-advance.ts. That module imports ONLY computeCableNetworkBalance from ./routes.js (economy-advance.ts:42) and never calls tickRoutes. A repo-wide grep shows tickRoutes' sole call site is the client render ticker src/main.ts:2556; it is absent from economy-advance.ts, runtime.ts, intent-runner.ts, and ws.ts. tickRoutes (src/routes.ts:805-828) is the single function that (a) delivers in-flight cargo (deliverArrivals), (b) dispatches new cargo from routes (dispatchPhase), and (c) prunes draining routes once inFlight is empty (lines 822-825). Because it never runs on the server: routes never move resources between islands server-side, never dispatch, and draining routes are never removed. The pre-migration build was client-only so main.ts's ticker covered it; the server-authoritative migration moved economy integration to advanceWorldEconomy but did not port the route tick, so the entire inter-island route subsystem is non-functional in the default REMOTE mode and delete-route's soft-delete can never complete. This is a behavior regression of REMOTE vs LOCAL (LOCAL still ticks routes via main.ts).
confirmed.evidence:
src/economy-advance.ts:42 `import { computeCableNetworkBalance } from './routes.js';` — only that symbol; `grep -n 'tickRoutes\|deliverArrivals\|dispatch' src/economy-advance.ts` returns nothing.
repo-wide: `grep -rn tickRoutes src server` -> only src/main.ts:2556 (call), src/routes.ts (def + tests), plus comments. No server call site.
server/src/game/runtime.ts:43 `advanceWorldEconomy(world, islandStates, now, now);` is the server's whole catch-up; no route tick before/after.
server/src/game/ws.ts: no route/tickRoutes/prune references (grep empty). server/src/game/intent-runner.ts: no Route references.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Decide and align with SPEC: either (1) call tickRoutes inside the server catch-up path (advanceWorldEconomy or runtime.catchUp, threading nowMs/elapsedSec/wallOffset consistently with how the offline gap is integrated) so routes deliver/dispatch/prune authoritatively in REMOTE; or (2) if routes are intentionally out of scope for the server, update SPEC.md and disable/grey route creation+deletion in REMOTE so code and spec match. Whichever path, the delete-route handler should still hard-remove a no-in-flight route immediately (companion finding) so deletion works even before any route tick runs.

confirmed.title: REMOTE set-cargo-floor-pct cannot CLEAR a floor — server rejects undefined sourceFloorPct that LOCAL treats as 'remove gate'
confirmed.description: The gateway method setCargoFloorPct(routeId, cargoIndex, sourceFloorPct?) takes an OPTIONAL sourceFloorPct, and `undefined` is the documented 'clear the source-floor gate' signal. The routes-UI floor input wires this directly: when the user empties the floor field, src/routes-ui.ts:892 computes `const pct = raw === '' ? undefined : ...` and calls `setCargoFloorPct(route.id, index, pct)`. The LOCAL gateway impl (src/mutation-gateway.ts:486-496) handles this correctly — when `sourceFloorPct === undefined` it rewrites the entry WITHOUT the sourceFloorPct field, removing the gate (matching the UI's own LOCAL branch at routes-ui.ts:897 which `delete`s the key). The REMOTE impl (src/mutation-gateway.ts:655-657) does `send('set-cargo-floor-pct', { routeId, cargoIndex, sourceFloorPct })`. Because server-client.ts:162 serializes via `JSON.stringify`, an `undefined` value drops the key entirely, so the payload arrives as `{ routeId, cargoIndex }` with no `sourceFloorPct`. The server handler (intents.ts:697-704) destructures `sourceFloorPct` (gets `undefined`) and at line 702 rejects: `if (typeof sourceFloorPct !== 'number' ...) return { ok:false, error:'sourceFloorPct must be 0..100' }`. Net effect: in REMOTE mode (the DEFAULT boot mode) a player CANNOT remove a source-floor gate once set — clearing the field silently fails server-side while the same action succeeds in LOCAL. This is a real LOCAL/REMOTE behavior divergence and a data-loss-of-intent (the clear is dropped). Additionally the server unconditionally always writes `sourceFloorPct` into the entry (intents.ts:709), so even a future set can never unset.
confirmed.evidence: intents.ts:702 `if (typeof sourceFloorPct !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(sourceFloorPct) || sourceFloorPct < 0 || sourceFloorPct > 100) return { ok:false, error:'sourceFloorPct must be 0..100' }`; mutation-gateway.ts:486-494 LOCAL: `sourceFloorPct === undefined ? { resourceId, weight } : { resourceId, weight, sourceFloorPct }`; routes-ui.ts:892 `const pct = raw === '' ? undefined : Math.min(100, Math.max(0, Number(raw)||0));`; server-client.ts:162 `socket!.send(JSON.stringify({ type, payload, seq: mySeq }))` (undefined key dropped).
confirmed.suggestedFix: Make the server handler accept the clear case to match LOCAL: treat a missing/undefined sourceFloorPct as 'remove the gate'. e.g. `const hasFloor = sourceFloorPct !== undefined; if (hasFloor && (typeof sourceFloorPct !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(sourceFloorPct) || sourceFloorPct < 0 || sourceFloorPct > 100)) return err; ... route.cargo[cargoIndex] = hasFloor ? { resourceId: entry.resourceId, weight: entry.weight, sourceFloorPct } : { resourceId: entry.resourceId, weight: entry.weight };`. Range validation still applies when a value IS supplied. Update SPEC.md route-cargo section to document that omitting sourceFloorPct clears the gate, keeping LOCAL and REMOTE identical.

confirmed.title: Tier-reset cooldown gate permanently locks in REMOTE: UI uses performance.now() against epoch-domain lastResetAt
confirmed.description:
The tier-reset UI compares a performance.now() clock against state.lastResetAt, but in REMOTE mode lastResetAt lives in the wall-clock epoch domain, not the performance.now() page-load domain. This produces a ~1.7e12 ms mismatch that permanently locks the reset button after the first reset and renders a garbage cooldown string.

Trace:
- LOCAL ground truth (correct): the LOCAL gateway and the UI fallback call executeTierReset(state, now) with now=performance.now() (skilltree-ui.ts:184, mutation-gateway.ts:430). lastResetAt is therefore minted in the performance.now() domain, serialized literally, and on loadWorld() deserializeWorld(snapshot, Date.now(), performance.now()) computes a perfShift that correctly remaps lastResetAt back into the new performance.now() domain (persistence.ts:1094). The UI's now=performance.now() matches. Cooldown works.
- REMOTE: the server stamps lastResetAt = now where now = Date.now() (ws.ts:184 applyIntent(..., Date.now()) -> intent-runner.ts:56 handler.apply(game, payload, now) -> intents.ts:835 executeTierReset(island.state, now)). It then serializes via serializeWorld(world, states, now, now) passing the SAME epoch now as BOTH nowWallMs and nowPerfMs (server/src/game/ws.ts pushState / intent-runner.ts:75). So savedAt == savedAtPerf == epoch, and lastResetAt == epoch.
- Client applyRemoteSnapshot calls deserializeWorld(snapshot, Date.now(), Date.now()) (main.ts:2099-2100). perfShift = nowPerfMs - savedAtPerf - deltaMs = Date.now()_client - Date.now()_server - ~0 ~= 0, so lastResetAt stays in epoch (~1.7e12). No code re-normalizes lastResetAt into the perf domain (verified across applyRemoteSnapshot, main.ts:2098-2174).
- The economy/inspector in REMOTE run on the epoch domain too (lastTick = nowPerfMs - deltaMs = epoch; refreshRetainedRates(nowWall) with nowWall=Date.now()), so they are self-consistent. But skilltree-ui.ts hardcodes performance.now() (line 159 for the click handler's canTierReset + gateway.tierReset call, line 235 for refreshTierReset's canTierReset + cooldown display).

Failure path: First reset works (fresh island lastResetAt===null, the null-guard at tier-reset.ts:97 skips the cooldown branch). After it, the server snapshot carries lastResetAt in epoch (~1.7e12). canTierReset(state, performance.now()) computes nowMs - lastResetAt ~= 5e4 - 1.7e12 = hugely negative < TIER_RESET_COOLDOWN_MS, so it returns {ok:false, reason:'cooldown-active'} forever (the mismatch is ~54 years, never elapses). The button click handler hits 'if (!r.ok) return' (line 161) and silently no-ops; refreshTierReset paints 'COOLDOWN' permanently and shows a nonsense remaining time (h = floor((8.64e7 - (5e4 - 1.7e12))/3.6e6), ~millions of hours). A REMOTE player who resets once can never reset that island again, even after the real 24h cooldown.
confirmed.evidence: src/skilltree-ui.ts:159 `const now = performance.now();` then 160 `const r = canTierReset(state, now);` and 178 `await deps.gateway.tierReset(state.id, now);`. src/skilltree-ui.ts:235 `const now = performance.now();` then 237 `canTierReset(state, now)` and 240 `const elapsed = now - state.lastResetAt;`. Server stamps epoch: server/src/game/ws.ts:184 `applyIntent(pool, userId, parsed.env, Date.now())`; server/src/game/intents.ts:835 `executeTierReset(island.state, now)`. Client deserialize keeps epoch: src/main.ts:2099-2100 `const nowWall = Date.now(); const d = deserializeWorld(snapshot, nowWall, nowWall);` and persistence.ts:948 `const perfShift = nowPerfMs - snapshot.savedAtPerf - deltaMs;` (~0 here) with persistence.ts:1094 `lastResetAt: s.lastResetAt === null ? null : s.lastResetAt + perfShift`. tier-reset.ts:98 `nowMs - state.lastResetAt < TIER_RESET_COOLDOWN_MS`.
confirmed.suggestedFix: The tier-reset UI must use the same clock domain the live state is in. In REMOTE that is the wall-clock epoch (Date.now()); in LOCAL it is performance.now(). Thread the correct clock into skilltree-ui via deps (e.g. a deps.now()/clock callback that main.ts wires to Date.now() in REMOTE and performance.now() in LOCAL), and use it at both skilltree-ui.ts:159 and :235 instead of the hardcoded performance.now(). This mirrors how the REMOTE economy already uses nowWall=Date.now(). (The 'declaredAt'-based UI has the same latent domain hazard and should be swept with the same clock seam.)

confirmed.title: set-cargo-floor-pct intent rejects clearing a source-floor gate — REMOTE players cannot remove a floor% once set (LOCAL can)
confirmed.description: The routes-UI floor% input lets the player CLEAR the source-floor gate by emptying the field: routes-ui.ts:892 computes `const pct = raw === '' ? undefined : ...` and calls `deps.gateway.setCargoFloorPct(route.id, index, pct)` with pct possibly undefined. LOCAL handles undefined by rebuilding the cargo entry WITHOUT sourceFloorPct, i.e. removing the gate (mutation-gateway.ts:491-494: `sourceFloorPct === undefined ? { resourceId, weight } : { ..., sourceFloorPct }`). The REMOTE gateway forwards it as `send('set-cargo-floor-pct', { routeId, cargoIndex, sourceFloorPct })` (mutation-gateway.ts:656); with sourceFloorPct === undefined, JSON.stringify drops the key, so the server sees sourceFloorPct absent. The server handler (intents.ts:702) does `if (typeof sourceFloorPct !== 'number' || ... ) return { ok:false, error:'sourceFloorPct must be 0..100' }` — so a clear is REJECTED, and intents.ts:709 unconditionally writes `sourceFloorPct` into the entry, having no remove branch at all. Net effect in the default REMOTE mode: a player who sets a floor% can never clear it through the UI (the intent errors), and there is no server path to remove the gate — a behavioral divergence from LOCAL on a real interactive affordance. The prior audit's parity_note explicitly claimed 'the other 27 intents match'; this one does not.
confirmed.evidence: routes-ui.ts:892 `const pct = raw === '' ? undefined : Math.min(100, Math.max(0, Number(raw) || 0));` then :894 `await deps.gateway.setCargoFloorPct(route.id, index, pct);` (pct can be undefined). LOCAL clears the gate: mutation-gateway.ts:491-494. REMOTE forwards undefined (key dropped by JSON.stringify): mutation-gateway.ts:656. Server rejects undefined and has no clear branch: intents.ts:702 `if (typeof sourceFloorPct !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(sourceFloorPct) || sourceFloorPct < 0 || sourceFloorPct > 100) return { ok:false, error:'sourceFloorPct must be 0..100' };` and :709 always writes sourceFloorPct.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Mirror LOCAL's clear semantics server-side: in the set-cargo-floor-pct handler accept `sourceFloorPct === undefined` (validate it as 'number-or-undefined'), and when undefined rebuild the entry without sourceFloorPct (`route.cargo[cargoIndex] = { resourceId: entry.resourceId, weight: entry.weight }`) exactly as mutation-gateway.ts:491-494 does. Add a WS-path test asserting that sending no/undefined sourceFloorPct removes the gate and returns ok:true.

confirmed.title: Read-only state pushes never checkpoint, so an idle connected socket re-integrates an ever-growing offline gap every 1s (unbounded CPU growth)
confirmed.description: e2560bc split catch-up into a persisting path (loadAndCatchUp, used only by intents/new/import) and a read-only path (catchUp, used by the 1 Hz WS push and GET /api/game/state). The periodic push now calls catchUp(loadSnapshot(...), now) WITHOUT persisting. Before this commit, every push ran loadAndCatchUp which wrote the advanced snapshot back, so the stored savedAt advanced ~1s each push and the next push only had to integrate ~1s. Now the stored snapshot's savedAt is frozen until the NEXT accepted intent. For a connected-but-idle account (socket open, no intents — e.g. a player AFK, or just watching production), every 1 Hz push re-integrates the FULL gap since the last intent: after an hour idle each push re-runs a 1h economy catch-up; after a day, a full-day catch-up — every second, per connection. advanceWorldEconomy/advanceIsland integrate event-by-event (findNextCapEvent loop), so per-push cost grows with the idle gap. The fix comment justifies dropping the write to 'not widen the lost-update window / amplify writes', but traded a bounded 1s integration for an unbounded one with no periodic checkpoint anywhere (grep confirms saveSnapshot is reached only by new/import/intent).
confirmed.evidence: ws.ts:85 `const game = catchUp(await loadSnapshot(pool, userId), now);` inside the setInterval(STATE_PUSH_INTERVAL_MS=1000) push chain (ws.ts:144). catchUp (runtime.ts:24) calls advanceWorldEconomy and returns WITHOUT saveSnapshot. The only saveSnapshot callsites are routes.ts:40/69 (new/import) and intent-runner.ts:75 (accepted intent). No periodic/background persist exists.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Reintroduce a bounded checkpoint for the read path: either (a) have the periodic push persist the advanced snapshot at most every N seconds (debounced) under the per-account advisory lock so idle accounts still advance their stored savedAt, or (b) cap/curtail the push integration by persisting on the FIRST push after a long gap. The lost-update concern is addressed by keeping intents authoritative; a periodic checkpoint of read-advanced state is idempotent (same now → same state) and does not lose an unpersisted mutation because reads carry no mutation.

confirmed.title: No per-account WebSocket connection cap — many concurrent sockets (incl. CSWSH-driven) multiply the 20/s per-connection limit into an aggregate DB-write / lock-contention DoS on one saves row
confirmed.description: The prior fix added a per-CONNECTION sliding-window limiter (WS_INTENT_RATE_LIMIT=20/s) and an abuse-close threshold, but the limit is strictly per-socket and there is no cap on how many sockets one account (or the same userId across many sockets) may hold open. Each accepted intent runs `withAccountTx` which serializes ALL of that account's sockets through `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(userId))` and writes the full jsonb snapshot. So N open sockets for one account each push up to 20 intents/s → ~20·N serialized load-apply-persist transactions/s contending on a single saves row, plus N periodic 1 Hz pushState ticks, queueing behind the advisory lock and starving the legitimate tab. Because the Origin hole (finding above) lets a drive-by page open sockets with the victim's cookie, an attacker can open many hijacked sockets without the victim's involvement. The per-connection limiter cannot bound aggregate load because nothing limits connection count.
confirmed.evidence: ws.ts:152 `const limiter = new SlidingWindowLimiter(opts.intentRateLimit ?? WS_INTENT_RATE_LIMIT, ...)` is constructed fresh per connection inside the route handler (one instance/socket). No connection registry / per-user counter exists (grep for maxConnections/connCount/perAccount returns nothing). withAccountTx (db.ts:35) serializes every socket of the account on one advisory lock; saveSnapshot writes the whole snapshot per accepted intent (intent-runner.ts:75).
confirmed.suggestedFix: Track open sockets per userId in a module-level Map and refuse (close 1008) or evict the oldest when a single account exceeds a small cap (e.g. 4 concurrent sockets). Optionally share one rate-limit bucket per userId rather than per connection so aggregate intent throughput is bounded regardless of socket count.

confirmed.title: Inspector 'Convert to Servitor' (Eternal Servitor, §4.7/§13.3) bypasses the mutation-gateway — broken in REMOTE default mode, reverted within ~1s
confirmed.description: The inspector's 'Convert to Servitor' button click handler calls the pure `convertToServitor(target.state, target.building.id, BUILDING_DEFS)` DIRECTLY on local island state, with no `deps.gateway` routing — unlike every other inspector mutation (e.g. relabelCargo at inspector-ui.ts:792-804 and the floor steppers at 1011-1012, which dispatch through the gateway). The `MutationGateway` interface (src/mutation-gateway.ts:100-) has NO convertToServitor method at all, and the server intent map intentionally leaves `convert-to-servitor` UNWIRED (server/src/game/intents.ts:962-966 documents this: importing buildings.ts would drag PixiJS into the server). So in REMOTE mode (the default boot mode) clicking Convert mutates the client's local copy of the island state, the inspector paints success (line 957-958), the Servitor Conversion Kit is visually consumed locally — but the mutation is NEVER sent to the server. On the very next server snapshot (applyRemoteSnapshot pushes every ~1s and on every intent), `islandStates.set(id, st)` (main.ts:2125-2127) replaces the locally-mutated state object with the authoritative server version, silently reverting the conversion (and the kit consumption). Eternal Servitor is a fully-implemented T5 endgame mechanic per SPEC §13.3 (line 1657) and §8.4 — it is non-functional in the default mode with deceptive success feedback. The prior parity sweep enumerated only the 30 WIRED intents' payload-field parity; it structurally missed UI mutations that bypass the gateway entirely.
confirmed.evidence: src/inspector-ui.ts:956 `const res = convertToServitor(target.state, target.building.id, BUILDING_DEFS);` — direct pure call, no gateway. Contrast inspector-ui.ts:792 `deps.gateway ? deps.gateway.relabelCargo(...)`. MutationGateway interface (mutation-gateway.ts:100-) has no convertToServitor / servitor method. server/src/game/intents.ts:962 `// UNWIRED: convert-to-servitor.` Revert path: main.ts:2125-2127 `for (const [id, st] of d.islandStates) { islandStates.set(id, st); }` replaces state every snapshot. SPEC.md:1657 documents the live mechanic.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Extract the pure servitor-conversion logic out of the PixiJS-importing buildings.ts into a renderer-free module (mirroring the pure/render split AGENTS.md mandates), add a `convertToServitor(islandId, buildingId)` method to MutationGateway (LOCAL → pure call; REMOTE → 'convert-to-servitor' intent), wire a server intent that re-validates Reality-Forge presence + kit recipe and applies the conversion to authoritative state, and route the inspector button through the gateway with the gatewayResult-await pattern used elsewhere. Until wired, disable/hide the Convert button in REMOTE so it doesn't present a deceptive success.

confirmed.title: Inspector Universe Editor biome reassignment (§13.3) bypasses the mutation-gateway — broken in REMOTE default mode, reverted within ~1s
confirmed.description: The Universe Editor biome-reassign buttons in the inspector call the pure `editIslandBiome(deps.world, tspec.id, b.id)` DIRECTLY (inspector-ui.ts:1900) with no gateway routing. There is no gateway method for biome reassignment and no server intent. In REMOTE (the default), clicking a biome button re-rolls terrain and wipes/re-rolls modifiers on the LOCAL world copy, deducts UNIVERSE_EDITOR_COST locally, calls onIslandBiomeReassigned + paint() to show success — but nothing reaches the server, so the next ~1s server snapshot reverts the biome, terrain, modifiers, and cost via the in-place Object.assign(worldState, d.world) (main.ts:2115) plus the islandStates Map reconciliation. Universe Editor is a fully-implemented T5 mechanic per SPEC §8.9 (catalog row), §13.2-13.3 (line 55), and the detailed §reality-editing note (SPEC.md:1645). Same gateway-bypass class as the servitor finding; both were missed because the prior sweep only audited the 30 wired intents, not UI calls that skip the gateway.
confirmed.evidence: src/inspector-ui.ts:1900 `const r = editIslandBiome(deps.world, tspec.id, b.id);` inside the button click handler (1891) — direct pure mutation of deps.world, no `deps.gateway`. MutationGateway interface has no biome/universe-editor method. No server intent named for editIslandBiome (grep over server/src/game/intents.ts shows none). Revert: main.ts:2115 `Object.assign(worldState, d.world);` overwrites islands (incl. biome/terrain/modifiers) every snapshot. SPEC.md:1645 documents the live mechanic with its real cost.
confirmed.suggestedFix: Add a gateway method (e.g. `editBiome(islandId, biomeId)`; LOCAL → editIslandBiome; REMOTE → a new 'edit-biome' intent) and a server intent that re-validates Universe Editor presence + UNIVERSE_EDITOR_COST affordability and applies editIslandBiome to authoritative world state (universe-editor.ts is pure — no PixiJS — so it can be imported server-side directly, unlike convertToServitor). Route the inspector buttons through the gateway. Until wired, disable the buttons in REMOTE.

confirmed.title: dispatch-repair-drone / move-satellite deduct propellant and mutate sat state, but their completion ticks (tickRepairDrones / tickSatMovement) never run server-side — repair never lands and the sat is permanently blocked from re-repair
